commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=29bd310547cdb361718812b3dbd36f772aabe987)
commands.review_summary:
The branch removes Telegram DM thread reply configuration, derives DM topic session splitting from `getMe.has_topics_enabled`, and adds doctor cleanup, docs, metadata, changelog, and tests for that path.

PR surface: Source +78, Tests +127, Docs +1, Generated 0. Total +206 across 21 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main defaults omitted DM thread handling to flat sessions and honors explicit DM threading config, while the PR moves that decision to bot capability detection. I did not see live Telegram proof reproducing the DM topic runtime behavior.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is the product and compatibility decision around retiring explicit Telegram DM threading overrides and startup cache behavior.

target.title: fix(telegram): derive DM topics from bot capability
target.body:
## Summary
- Remove Telegram DM thread reply policy from validated config, generated metadata, UI hints, and docs.
- Derive DM topic session splitting only from Telegram `getMe.has_topics_enabled` at message and command runtime.
- Keep deprecated SDK type compatibility and add `openclaw doctor --fix` migration for retired `dm` / `threadReplies` keys.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-threads.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel.gateway.test.ts`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/doctor-contract.ts extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Telegram DM topic sessions no longer use a user-facing `threadReplies` config switch; they split only when Telegram `getMe.has_topics_enabled` is true, and doctor removes retired `dm` / `threadReplies` keys from existing configs.
Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created temp configs with `channels.telegram.enabled=false`, first without retired settings, then with `dm.threadReplies`, `direct.<chatId>.threadReplies`, account-scoped equivalents, and empty legacy `dm: {}` stanzas; ran `pnpm openclaw config validate --json`, `pnpm openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes`, then `pnpm openclaw config validate --json` again under the temp env.
Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the isolated temp OpenClaw setup:

```text
valid-without-setting={"valid":true,"path":"/var/folders/.../state/openclaw.json"}
invalid-before=failed-as-expected
{
  "valid": false,
  "issues": [
    { "path": "channels.telegram", "message": "invalid config: must NOT have additional properties" },
    { "path": "channels.telegram.direct.123", "message": "invalid config: must NOT have additional properties" },
    { "path": "channels.telegram.accounts.work", "message": "invalid config: must NOT have additional properties" },
    { "path": "channels.telegram.accounts.work.direct.456", "message": "invalid config: must NOT have additional properties" }
  ]
}
doctor-output=Removed channels.telegram.dm.threadReplies.
doctor-output=Removed channels.telegram.direct.123.threadReplies.
doctor-output=Removed channels.telegram.accounts.work.dm.threadReplies.
doctor-output=Removed channels.telegram.accounts.work.direct.456.threadReplies.
valid-after-doctor={"valid":true,"path":"/var/folders/.../state/openclaw.json"}
empty-dm-invalid-before=failed-as-expected
empty-dm-doctor-changes=Removed channels.telegram.dm.
empty-dm-doctor-changes=Removed channels.telegram.accounts.work.dm.
empty-dm-valid-after={"valid":true,"path":"/var/folders/.../state/openclaw.json"}
empty-dm-repaired-config={"enabled":false,"accounts":{"work":{"enabled":false,"dmPolicy":"pairing","groupPolicy":"allowlist"}},"dmPolicy":"pairing","groupPolicy":"allowlist"}
```

Observed result after fix: removed keys no longer validate as Telegram config surface, `doctor --fix` deletes retired legacy config, and tests show runtime session keys depend on `has_topics_enabled`.
What was not tested: live Telegram Bot API; no real bot token was used.

Refs #86513.
Alternative to #86542.
target.author: alexph-dev

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
